title: Tender Malty Korean Mint Anju
description: A malty and tender Korean dish from Jeju, featuring fresh mint and a plant-based friendly recipe, perfect for a cozy evening. This anju dish is cut in the traditional katsuramuki style.
instructions: Cut the tofu into small cubes and set aside.
Grind the malted barley flour into a fine powder using a blender or food processor.
Heat the sesame oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic to the pan and saute until fragrant.
Add the tofu cubes to the pan and cook until golden brown.
Add the soy sauce, rice vinegar, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the powdered malted barley flour to the pan and stir to combine.
Chop the fresh Korean mint leaves and add them to the pan.
Stir everything together and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the anju hot, garnished with additional mint leaves if desired.
